bibliographic_data.title: Segmentation Guided Image-to-Image Translation with Adversarial Networks
bibliographic_data.abstract: Recently image-to-image translation has received increasing attention, which aims to map images in one domain to another specific one. Existing methods mainly solve this task via a deep generative model and focus on exploring the relationship between different domains. However, these methods neglect to utilize higher-level and instance-specific information to guide the training process, leading to a great deal of unrealistic generated images of low quality. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Segmentation Guided Generative Adversarial Networks (SGGAN), which leverages semantic segmentation to further boost the generation performance and provide spatial mapping.

core_content.novelty_claim: A novel Segmentation Guided Generative Adversarial Networks (SGGAN) is proposed, which leverages semantic segmentation to guide the image translation process and improve image quality.
core_content.key_methodology: The SGGAN framework consists of a generator, a discriminator with an auxiliary classifier, and a segmentor. The segmentor network provides spatial guidance to ensure generated images comply with input segmentations, while the discriminator pushes generated images towards the target domain distribution.
outcomes: Empirically demonstrated superior image quality over several state-of-the-art methods on multi-domain face image translation tasks.
Provided spatial controllability during translation and interpretable results.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Image-to-image translation aims to map an image in a source domain to its corresponding image in a target domain, which generalizes various computer vision tasks such as image super-resolution, semantic segmentation, style transfer, and face recognition. Recent advancements in generative adversarial networks (GAN) have significantly improved the field of deep generative models for image translation. However, existing methods still face challenges related to reliability and spatial controllability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Segmentation Guided Generative Adversarial Networks (SGGAN), which leverages semantic segmentation to guide the generation process and improve image quality. The SGGAN framework includes three networks: a generator, a discriminator with an auxiliary classifier, and a segmentor. The segmentor network provides spatial guidance to ensure generated images comply with input segmentations, while the discriminator pushes generated images towards the target domain distribution.
